sectiondef.header: Protocol Selection definitions : DP 1.1, DP 1.2, DP 1.4.
DP generic definitions: Link bandwidth and lane count.
DP generic definitions: Bits per color components.
DP generic definitions: Miscellaneous components; color format.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Receiver capability field.

description.para: Address mapping for the DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) of the downstream device.

briefdescription.para: Number of receiver ports and power voltage capability.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link configuration field.

briefdescription.para: FAUX back channel symbol error count.
FAUX back channel training pattern time.
RX GTC value phase skew enable.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link/sink status field.

briefdescription.para: FAUX forward channel symbol error count.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Automated testing sub-field.

briefdescription.para: FAUX back channel symbol error count control.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Sink control field.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Sideband message buffers.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Event status indicator field.

briefdescription.para: Sink device service IRQ vector ESI0.
Sink device service IRQ vector ESI1.
Sink link service IRQ vector ESI0.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Extended Configuration Data Registers.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Field addresses and sizes.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Receiver capability field masks,

briefdescription.para: Shift bits for DPCD revision major.
Post link training adjust request support mask.
VSC SDP extension for colorimetry supported.
No AUX handshake link training mask.
Number of receiver ports type 1RP mask.
Number of receiver ports type G2RP mask.
Shift bits for downstream port type.
Downstream port detailed capability info available mask.
RX port local EDID present mask.
RX port associated to preceding port mask.
RX port horizontal black expansion capable mask.
RX port buffer size unit mask.
RX port buffer size per port mask.
Training AUX read extended RX capability field present mask.
Adaptor capability force load sense mask.
Adaptor capability alternate I2C pattern mask.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor mask.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 3 ms.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 2 ms.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 1 ms.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 500 us.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 200 us.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 100 us.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 10 us.
AV sync data block audio granularity factor 1 us.
AV sync data block video granularity factor mask.
AV sync data block video granularity factor 3 ms.
AV sync data block video granularity factor 2 ms.
AV sync data block video granularity factor 1 ms.
AV sync data block video granularity factor 500 us.
AV sync data block video granularity factor 200 us.
AV sync data block video granularity factor 100 us.
AV sync data block audio decoder latency mask.
AV sync data block audio processing latency mask.
AV sync data block video interlaced latency mask.
AV sync data block video progressive latency mask.
AV sync data block repeater latency mask.
AV sync data block audio delay insertion mask.
TX GTC value phase skew enable mask.
RX GTC frequency lock done mask.
DSC RC buffer block size mask.
DSC 1 slice per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 2 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 4 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 6 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 8 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 10 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 12 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC line buffer bit depth mask.
DSC line buffer bit depth 9 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 10 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 11 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 12 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 13 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 14 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 15 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 16 bits.
DSC line buffer bit depth 8 bits.
DSC decoder RGB color format capability mask.
DSC decoder YCrCb 4:4:4 color format capability mask.
DSC decoder YCrCb 4:2:2 simple color format capability mask.
DSC decoder YCrCb 4:2:2 native color format capability mask.
DSC decoder YCrCb 4:2:0 native color format capability mask.
DSC decoder 8 bits per color capability mask.
DSC decoder 10 bits per color capability mask.
DSC decoder 12 bits per color capability mask.
Peak DSC throughput mode not supported.
DSC 16 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 20 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC 24 slices per DP DSC sink device mask.
DSC bits per pixel increment mask.
DSC bits per pixel increment 1/16 bpp.
DSC bits per pixel increment 1/8 bpp.
DSC bits per pixel increment 1/4 bpp.
DSC bits per pixel increment 1/2 bpp.
DSC bits per pixel increment 1 bpp.
Downstream port maximum bits per color mask.
Downstream port maximum 8 bits per color.
Downstream port maximum 10 bits per color.
Downstream port maximum 12 bits per color.
Downstream port maximum 16 bits per color.
Downstream port HDMI/DP++ frame sequential to frame pack mask.
Downstream port DVI high color depth mask.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link configuration field masks,

briefdescription.para: Shift bits for link quality pattern.
Shift bits for symbol error count select.
Disparity error and illegal symbol error.
MSA timing parameters ignored enable mask.
Maximum post-cursor2 for lane 0/2 reached mask.
Maximum post-cursor2 for lane 1/3 reached mask.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link/sink status field masks, shifts,

briefdescription.para: Shift bits for sink count high/low.
Voltage swing adjust request for lane 0/2 mask.
Pre-emphasis adjust request for lane 0/2 mask.
Voltage swing adjust request for lane 1/3 mask.
Shift bits for voltage swing lane 1/3.
Pre-emphasis adjust request for lane 1/3 mask.
Post-cursor2 adjust request for lane 0 mask.
Post-cursor2 adjust request for lane 1 mask.
Post-cursor2 adjust request for lane 2 mask.
Post-cursor2 adjust request for lane 3 mask.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Field addresses and sizes.

description.para: Address mapping for the Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) of the downstream device.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Register offsets for the

briefdescription.para: Vertical front porch/sync width lower 4 bits.
Horizontal/vertical front porch/sync width upper 2 bits.
Horizontal image size in mm LSB.
Vertical image size in mm LSB.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Masks, shifts, and register

briefdescription.para: Shift bits for resolution upper bits.
Vertical sync pulse width lower bits mask.
Vertical front porch lower bits mask.
Shift bits for vertical front porch.
Horizontal front porch upper bits mask.
Horizontal sync pulse width upper bits mask.
Vertical front porch upper bits mask.
Vertical sync pulse width upper bits mask.
Shift bits for horizontal front porch upper bits.
Shift bits for horizontal sync width upper bits.
Shift bits for vertical front porch upper bits.
Vertical image size upper bits mask.
Horizontal image size upper bits mask.
Shift bits for horizontal image size upper bits.
Shift bits for horizontal sync polarity.
Shift bits for vertical sync polarity.

sectiondef.header: Stream identification.

briefdescription.para: Shift bits for audio control lane set.

sectiondef.header: Sideband message codes when the driver is in MST mode.
Register access macro definitions.

briefdescription.para: Macro for reading 32-bit values from a DisplayPort register address.
Macro for writing 32-bit values to a DisplayPort register address.
This typedef enumerates the RX and TX modes of operation for the DisplayPort core.
This typedef enumerates the dynamic ranges available to the DisplayPort core.
This typedef enumerates the handlers for the the DisplayPort Receiver.
This typedef enumerates the handlers for the the DisplayPort Transmitter.
Callback type which represents a custom timer wait handler.

detaileddescription.para: This is only used for Microblaze since it doesn't have a native sleep function. To avoid dependency on a hardware timer, the default wait functionality is implemented using loop iterations; this isn't too accurate. If a custom timer handler is used, the user may implement their own wait implementation using a hardware timer (see example/) for better accuracy.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the number of microseconds to be passed to the timer function.

briefdescription.para: Callback type which represents the handler for interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This table contains a list of global unique identifiers (GUIDs) that will be issued when exploring the topology using the algorithm in the XDp_TxFindAccessibleDpDevices function.
A table of configuration structures containing the configuration information for each DisplayPort TX core in the system.
This function retrieves the configuration for this DisplayPort instance and fills in the InstancePtr->Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a pointer to the configuration structure that will be used to copy the settings from.
is the device base address in the virtual memory space. If the address translation is not used, then the physical address is passed.

simplesect.para: Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function prepares the DisplayPort core for use depending on whether the core is operating in TX or RX mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the DisplayPort core was successfully initialized.

briefdescription.para: This function installs a custom delay/sleep function to be used by the XDp driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the address to the callback function.
is the user data item (microseconds to delay) that will be passed to the custom sleep/delay function when it is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function is the delay/sleep function for the XDp driver.

detaileddescription.para: For the Zynq family, there exists native sleep functionality. For MicroBlaze however, there does not exist such functionality. In the MicroBlaze case, the default method for delaying is to use a predetermined amount of loop iterations. This method is prone to inaccuracy and dependent on system configuration; for greater accuracy, the user may supply their own delay/sleep handler, pointed to by InstancePtr->UserTimerWaitUs, which may have better accuracy if a hardware timer is used.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the number of microseconds to delay/sleep for.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the link rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the link rate to check if valid.

listitem.para: 1 if specified link rate is valid.
0 otherwise, if the link rate specified isn't valid as per spec, or if it exceeds the capabilities of the TX core.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the lane count.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the number of lanes to check if valid.

listitem.para: 1 if specified lane count is valid.
0 otherwise, if the lane count specified isn't valid as per spec, or if it exceeds the capabilities of the TX core.

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XDp_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the XDp driver.

detaileddescription.para: When an interrupt happens, this interrupt handler will check which TX/RX mode of operation the core is running in, and will call the appropriate interrupt handler. The called interrupt handler will first detect what kind of interrupt happened, then decides which callback function to invoke.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the XDp driver/device depending on whether the core is operating in TX or RX mode.

detaileddescription.para: The sanity test checks whether or not all tested registers hold their default reset values.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the self-test passed - all tested registers hold their default reset values.

briefdescription.para: This is function determines whether the DisplayPort core, represented by the XDp structure pointed to, is a transmitter (TX) or a receiver (RX).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para: XDP_RX if the core is of type RX. XDP_TX if the core is of type TX.

briefdescription.para: The following functions set the color encoding scheme for a given stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the stream number for which to configure the color encoding scheme for.

simplesect.para: C-style signatures: u32 XDp_TxCfgSetRGB(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetSRGB(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYonly(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr422Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr422Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr444Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetYCbCr444Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc422Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc422Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc444Bt601(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetXvYcc444Bt709(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream) u32 XDp_TxCfgSetAdbRGB(XDp *InstancePtr, u8 Stream)

briefdescription.para: The number of I2C user- defined entries in the I2C map of each port.
The number of bytes for the global unique ID.
The maximum number of ports connected to a DisplayPort device.
This is a low-level function that reads from the specified register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the register offset to be read from.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value of the specified register.
C-style signature: u32 XDp_ReadReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This is a low-level function that writes to the specified register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the register offset to write to.
is the 32-bit data to write to the specified register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XDp_WriteReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Contains a minimal set of functions for the XDp driver that allow access to all of the DisplayPort core's functionality.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the stream policy maker functions for the XDp driver.
This file contains static initialization methods for the XDp driver.
This file contains a diagnostic self-test function for the XDp driver.
This file contains functions related to XDp interrupt handling.
This header file contains the identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains functions related to accessing the Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) of a specified sink using the XDp driver operating in TX mode.
See xdp.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Updated RX initialization with MST support. Added callbacks for lane count changes, link rate changes and pre-emphasis + voltage swing adjust requests. 5.1 als 08/09/16 Replaced deprecated MB_Sleep with consolidated usleep. 08/11/16 RX to support maximum pre-emphasis level of 1. 08/12/16 Updates to support 64-bit base addresses. 5.2 aad 01/21/17 Added training timeout disable for RX MST mode for soft-disconnect to work. 6.0 tu 05/14/17 Added AUX defer to 6 6.0 jb 02/19/19 Added HDCP22 functions. 02/21/19 Added returning AUX defers for HDCP22 DPCD offsets 6.0 jb 08/22/19 Removed returning AUX defers for HDCP22 DPCD offsets 7.4 rg 09/01/20 Added XDp_TxColorimetryVsc API for reading sink device capability for receiving colorimetry information through VSC SDP packets.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver.

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xdp.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Added MST registers, masks, and values for RX. Added HDCP registers and masks. 5.0 als 05/16/16 Added MISC0 and MISC1 definitions. 5.1 aad 08/16/16 Updated MISC0 definitions. 5.2 aad 01/21/17 Added timeout macro for training timeout 6.0 tu 05/14/17 Added AUX defer shift mask 6.0 tu 08/03/17 Enabled video packing for bpc > 10 6.0 tu 08/24/17 Modify #define for YCBCR422 and YCBCR444 6.0 jb 02/19/19 Added HDCP22 registers.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Added MST interrupt handlers for RX. Guard against uninitialized callbacks. Added HDCP interrupts. Added unplug interrupt. 4.0 als 02/18/16 Removed update of payload table in the driver's interrupt handler. 6.0 tu 05/30/17 Removed unused variable in XDp_RxInterruptHandler 6.0 tu 09/08/17 Added two interrupt handler that addresses driver's internal callback function of application DrvHpdEventHandler and DrvHpdPulseHandler 6.0 tu 09/08/17 Added three interrupt handler that addresses callback function of application 6.0 jb 02/19/19 Added HDCP22 interrupts. 7.5 rg 08/19/20 Added a interrupt handler that addresses driver's internal callback function of application ExtPktCallbackHandler 7.5 rg 03/25/21 Add support for adaptive-sync in MST mode

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 06/08/15 Added MST functionality to RX. 4.0 als 02/16/16 XDp_TxAllocatePayloadStreams now allocates payloads by using an internally calculated starting timeslot for each stream rather than invoking XDp_TxGetFirstAvailableTs. XDp_TxAllocatePayloadVcIdTable now takes an additional argument (StartTs, the starting timeslot). Exposed RX MST API to application: XDp_RxAllocatePayloadStream 5.2 aad 01/24/16 XDp_RxAllocatePayloadStream now adjusts to timeslot rearragement 6.0 tu 05/30/17 Initialized variable in XDp_RxDeviceInfoToRawData

detaileddescription.para: It will check many of the DisplayPort core's register values against the default reset values as a sanity-check that the core is ready to be used.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 5.0 als 07/27/16 8B10B enable is now set by default in PHY_CONFIG reg. 6.0 tu 08/04/17 Changed Selftest regiter's default value.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 6.0 tu 02/06/17 Initialize CfgPtr to NULL.

detaileddescription.para: These functions set up the DisplayPort TX core's main stream attributes (MSA) that determine how a video stream will be displayed and also some DisplayPort RX MSA-related functions.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial release. TX code merged from the dptx driver. 2.0 als 07/27/15 Scale TX fractional register by 1024 instead of 1000. 3.0 als 10/07/15 Added MSA callback. 4.0 als 02/07/16 Enable/disable end of line reset for reduced blanking. 5.0 als 05/16/16 Added API to set color encoding scheme. 5.1 als 08/03/16 Use video common API rather than internal structure when checking for interlaced mode. als 08/12/16 Updates to support 64-bit base addresses. 5.2 aad 01/24/17 Disable end of line reset for reduced blanking 6.0 tu 07/20/17 Allowing Custom VTM in XDp_TxCfgMsaUseStandardVideoMode function. 6.0 aad 09/05/17 Reverted to enable end of line reset for RB resolutions. 6.0 tu 09/06/17 Added Set UserPixelWidth support on tx side 7.4 rg 09/26/20 Added support for YUV420 color format.
